title: panicum crus-galli var. stagninum
intro: Panicum crus-galli var. stagninum, also known as barnyard grass, is a perennial plant species native to Europe, Asia, and North Africa. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities. The plant is rich in flavonoids, saponins, and other bioactive compounds that have been reported to have various health benefits, including antioxidant, immunomodulatory, and cardiovascular-protective effects. In herbal preparations, barnyard grass is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to support respiratory health, alleviate digestive issues, and promote overall well-being.
